data.title: Supermicro prices $700M convertible senior notes
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) priced $700M aggregate principal amount of newly issued 2.25% convertible senior notes due 2028

The company also announced the amended pricing terms of its existing $1.725B aggregate convertible senior notes due 2029.

The amendments are expected to be effective, and the subscription is expected to close on or about February 20, 2025.

The company will receive gross proceeds of ~$700M and intends to use such proceeds for general corporate purposes, including to fund working capital for growth and business expansion.

It also announced the amended convertible notes will bear an annual interest rate of 3.50%, payable semi-annually on each March 1 and September 1, beginning on September 1, 2025.

data.title: Elon Musk to withdraw his $97.4B OpenAI offer if ChatGPT maker stays non-profit - report
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk intends to pull back his $97.4B bid for OpenAI's non-profit arm if the maker of ChatGPT does not become a for-profit entity, Reuters reported, citing Musk's lawyers in a court filing.

"If (the) OpenAI board is prepared to preserve the charity's mission and stipulate to take the "for sale" sign off its assets by halting its conversion, Musk will withdraw the bid," the filing said.

Otherwise, "the charity must be compensated by what an arms-length buyer will pay for its assets." The filing cited Musk's "serious offer" was made to enhance the charity's goals.

Earlier, Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, stated, "OpenAI is not for sale." at the AI Action Summit in Paris. "OpenAI's mission is not for sale - to say nothing of the fact that, like, a competitor who is not able to beat us in the market and you know, instead is just trying to say, like, 'I'm gonna buy this" with total disregard for the mission is a likely path there,” Altman added.

Musk sued Altman and others in August and requested U.S. district judge to block OpenAI's attempt to transition to a for-profit entity.

Musk reportedly said that the company's original mission of creating open-source technology that is not subject to corporate priorities has been undermined with Microsoft (MSFT) being the largest investor in OpenAI.

Tesla and OpenAI did not immediately reply to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: European markets extend gains amid U.S.-Russia talks
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.63% UK economy expands 0.1% in Q4 2024, estimates showed.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.87% DAX sets new all-time high; Germany's inflation eases to 2.3% in January.

France (CAC:IND) +0.68%

Romanian industrial output dropped 3.4% in December.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.30% on Thursday, maintaining a positive momentum from earlier this week, as traders assessed a host of earnings and economic data, and potential Ukraine-Russia peace talks.

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he had spoken to both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and that both leaders wanted peace.

On the corporate front, German technology conglomerate Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) was among top performers after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter profits.

Nestle's (OTCPK:NSRGY) full-year sales growth declined in line with expectations, while Commerzbank (OTCPK:CRZBF) said it would to cut 3,900 jobs as it unveils new targets.

Coming up in the session: Eurozone industrial production data awaited shortly,

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.60%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point at 2.47%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged to 4.54%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Bank of Canada rate cut reflects U.S. tariff threat, officials fear permanent GDP hit
data.description:
Bank of Canada officials decided to cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 3% last month to better balance inflation risks, and support the economy in light of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threat as "the adverse impact on the level of GDP would be permanent."

The policymakers agreed that U.S. tariffs "would weigh on business confidence and investment intentions, as well as consumer sentiment," according to minutes of the monetary policy meeting released on Wednesday.

While there has been some pick up in economic activity, with inflation around 2%, Bank of Canada warned that a protracted trade conflict would lead to weaker GDP and higher prices. The impact would be greater for Canada than the U.S.

"Even if no tariffs were imposed, a long period of uncertainty under the cloud of tariff threats would almost certainly damage business investment in Canada," the minutes stated, as companies are already re-evaluating their investment plans, raising the risk of capital flight from the country to the U.S.

A trade war would slow global growth, which Bank of Canada officials said would reduce energy demand and potentially lower oil prices. "This would tend to lower inflation, but it would also reduce incomes in Canada, given the country's large energy export sector," according to the minutes.

Earlier this month, Trump had announced 25% tariffs on most Canadian goods and 10% duties on energy imports. He later put the tariffs on hold till March 4 after Canada agreed to reinforce border security.

data.title: TEN Holdings announces pricing of $10.0M IPO
data.description:
• TEN Holdings (XHLD) on Thursday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of an aggregate of 1,667,000 shares of common stock for $6.00 per share.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol "XHLD" on February 13.
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $10.0 million from the offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 14, 2025.

data.title: UK economy expands 0.1% in Q4 2024, estimates showed
data.description:
The British economy expanded 0.1% in the final quarter of 2024, seeing no growth from the previous quarter, above estimates of a 0.1% contraction, preliminary estimates showed.

On the production side, the services sector increased by 0.2%, construction grew, by 0.5%, while production fell by 0.8%.

On the expenditure side, there were falls in expenditure terms in net trade and gross fixed capital formation that were offset by a large increase in change in inventories.

Monthly GDP in the United Kingdom increased to 0.40% M/M in December from 0.10% in November, a Y/Y surge of 1.5%.

ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Bell announces offering of $2.25 billion aggregate principal amount of hybrid notes
data.description:
Bell Canada, a BCE (NYSE:BCE) unit, Thursday announced the offering of $2.25 billion aggregate principal amounts of fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes in two series.

The $1 billion fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes, series A due 2055 will initially bear interest at an annual rate of 6.875% and reset every five years starting on September 15, 2030 at an annual rate equal to the five-year U.S. Treasury rate plus a spread of 2.390%.

The $1.25 billion fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes, series B due 2055 will initially bear interest at an annual rate of 7.000% and reset every five years starting on September 15, 2035 at an annual rate equal to the five-year U.S. Treasury rate plus a spread of 2.363%.

Closing of the offering is expected to occur on February 18, 2025. The notes will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by BCE (NYSE:BCE).
